summary:
Great! The fix works. The test confirms that the query count went down from what would have been >15 queries to only 2, indicating the DataLoader batching is now properly working. 

The issue was that previously, DataLoader instances were being created inside each resolver call instead of at the request level. This caused the batching functionality to fail because each resolver had its own DataLoader instance that only held a single request (the N+1 problem).

My solution involved:
1. Updating the context to include the shared DataLoader instances via the existing `createLoaders` function
2. Modifying the context type definition to include the loaders
3. Updating both the user and post resolvers to use the shared loaders from the context instead of creating new ones each time

This fixed the N+1 query problem while preserving all the functional behavior of the application.
